issue.title: Reconciler: bootstrap missing activeWork entries from active plan files (narrow rule)

successCriteria.description: A recent active plan with no activeWork entry bootstraps an issue:N entry at the artifact-derived step (planning/PLAN, planning/AUDIT when audited, or executing/EXECUTE with correct phase N/M when phases started)
No resurrection: an archived plan, history.recentIssues membership, or a stale plan (newest of createdAt/updatedAt/auditedAt older than 14 days) each independently block bootstrap
reconcile-state.mjs --print shows 'would create' rows for bootstrap candidates and remains strictly read-only
All 16 existing reconciler tests still pass; new tests cover every guard independently plus the positive bootstrap paths
Live-fire on this dogfood repo (~120 stale active plans, history only reaching #149): --print reports zero 'would create' rows and a real reconcile pass creates nothing

phases.title: Bootstrap rule in reconcile-state.mjs + guard tests
verification: Framework test suite green including all new bootstrap tests
Every guard has an independent TRAP test
phases.summary: Implemented findBootstrapCandidates(state, tikiPath, now) (exported, +BOOTSTRAP_RECENCY_MS=14d) with the 5 ordered guards (existing entry / history / archive presence / JSON-timestamp recency via newestPlanTimestamp / filename-content number match) and deriveTarget fallback {planning,PLAN} for phases-empty plans. Wired into reconcile()'s locked pass after the entry loop via applyTransition's create path; changes pushed as "bootstrapped at <STEP>". Safety-contract header rule 1 gained amendment 1a (cites ~120 stale plans, history reaching #149). 11 new tests (3 positive paths, 5 TRAP guards, no-op, dry-run, window-boundary 13d-in/15d-out) + re-pinned the stale-resurrection TRAP with explicit 45-day-old timestamps. Reconcile suite 27/27. Framework suite 84/85 — the 1 red is the dogfood-parity guard, EXPECTED until phase 2 regenerates .claude/tiki/scripts/ (live Stop-hook still runs the old reconciler; nothing ships from phase 1 alone). Pre-validated live-fire: findBootstrapCandidates on this repo's real .tiki returns ZERO candidates. testResults: { framework: "node", command: "pnpm -C packages/framework test", passed: 84, failed: 1, skipped: 0, status: "failed" } — failure is the expected phase-boundary dogfood drift, accepted by plan design.

phases.title: --print 'would create' mirror, dogfood regen, live-fire validation
phases.content:
1. Mirror the bootstrap rule in the read-only doctor: `buildReport(state, tikiPath)` in `packages/framework/scripts/reconcile-state.mjs` additionally calls `findBootstrapCandidates` and appends rows shaped like the existing ones with `recordedStatus/recordedStep/recordedPhase: null`, derived values from the candidate target, `drift: true`, `note: "would create (bootstrap)"`. `formatReport` needs no structural change (rows render generically) — verify the output reads sensibly and adjust only if needed. `--print` must remain strictly read-only (the existing test asserts this; extend it to cover a bootstrap candidate).
2. Tests: --print/report test with a bootstrap candidate → row present with note 'would create' and state file byte-unchanged; report with only excluded orphans (stale/archived/history) → no bootstrap rows.
3. Regenerate the dogfood copy: run `node packages/framework/install.js` from the repo root (the plugin-distribution test requires .claude/tiki/scripts/reconcile-state.mjs identical to canonical).
4. LIVE-FIRE on this repo (SC5): run `node .claude/tiki/scripts/reconcile-state.mjs --print` from the repo root — assert by inspection ZERO 'would create' rows despite ~120 stale active plans (all excluded by history/archive/recency guards). CAUTION: the live repo has issue:270 itself in activeWork mid-pipeline — its row must show as a normal entry, not a bootstrap row. Then run a real pass `node .claude/tiki/scripts/reconcile-state.mjs` (NOT --dry-run, NOT --quiet) and verify output changes:[] — nothing created, nothing advanced unexpectedly (270's recorded state may legitimately show drift healing toward its plan artifacts; if the pass advances issue:270 per its own plan file that is CORRECT reconciler behavior — report it, don't fight it).
5. Full framework suite green; then `pnpm -C packages/framework test` final count reported.
verification: --print shows 'would create' rows in fixture and is byte-level read-only
Live-fire --print on dogfood repo: zero bootstrap rows
Live-fire real pass: changes [] (or only legitimate self-healing of in-flight entries)
Dogfood script copy regenerated; plugin-distribution dogfood-parity test green
phases.summary: buildReport() now mirrors the bootstrap rule: one "would create (bootstrap #270)" row per findBootstrapCandidates result (null recorded triple renders as -/-, drift:true); --print stays strictly read-only (byte-unchanged state asserted in test). +2 tests (candidate row + state byte-unchanged; excluded orphans yield zero rows). Dogfood .claude/tiki/scripts/reconcile-state.mjs regenerated via install.js (settings.json line-ending churn restored). LIVE-FIRE SC5 on this repo: --print showed issue:270 as a normal in-sync row (executing/EXECUTE 2/2) and ZERO would-create rows despite ~120 stale active plans; real pass returned changes: [] verbatim. Framework suite 87/87 including the dogfood-parity guard. testResults: { framework: "node", command: "pnpm -C packages/framework test", passed: 87, failed: 0, skipped: 0, status: "passed" }. Ship note: canonical + dogfood copies must ship in the same commit or the parity guard goes red.
